conversation.content:
"You are playing a game involving a split of resources. 
Player RED starts with Dollars: 100, Player BLUE has none to trade.

RULES:
```

1. Player RED has 4 moves while Player BLUE has 4 moves.

2. You must either:

    A) Accept the current proposal by saying:
        <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    B) Reject the current proposal, whereby both players lose all their resources and the game ends immediately:
        <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Propose a new trade:
        <player answer> NONE </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: amount | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </newly proposed trade>

    Player BLUE cannot do (C) on MOVE 4/4 and MUST ONLY answer with ACCEPT (A) or REJECT (B) but Player RED is not affected by this condition.        
    
    Note: the game ends immediately if you ACCEPT or REJECT.
    
3. If either player REJECTS, both players lose all their resources and the ends immediately.

4. There are no future games.

5. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:
<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want
This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

6. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:
<message>your message here</message>
You can disclose anything in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Resources available in the game: Dollars
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<my name> [add here] </my name>
<MOVE> [add here] / [add here]  </MOVE> 
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<message> [add here] </message
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my name> RED </my name>
<MOVE> 1 / 4 </MOVE>
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
<reason> I start with 100 Dollars and Player BLUE has 0. Since BLUE cannot propose trades (except on their last move), I have the initiative. I can propose any trade, but since BLUE has nothing to give, any trade I propose will be one-sided: I give some dollars to BLUE, who gives nothing in return. However, since I have no incentive to give away resources unless I expect BLUE to reciprocate (which they can't), I should consider whether to propose a trade at all. If I propose a trade, BLUE must either accept or reject. If BLUE rejects, both lose everything — which is catastrophic for me. So, I should consider whether to propose a trade or just accept the status quo. But the rules say I must either accept, reject, or propose. Since I have no reason to reject (as I’m the only one with resources), and I can’t do nothing, I must propose something. The safest proposal is to give 0 dollars — effectively saying “no trade,” which BLUE will likely accept. But if BLUE accepts, then the trade is “NONE,” and the game continues. If BLUE rejects, I lose everything. So, I should propose a minimal trade that BLUE can accept — perhaps giving 1 dollar, which is better than nothing. But since BLUE has nothing to give, any trade I propose is essentially a gift. I can also consider whether to just accept the status quo — but the rules don’t allow that. I must choose one of the three options. Since I have the advantage, I should propose a trade that gives me the most leverage. Giving 0 dollars to BLUE (and asking for nothing) is the safest — it’s a “no trade” proposal, which BLUE will likely accept. If BLUE accepts, the game continues, and I retain my 100 dollars. If BLUE rejects, I lose everything — which is a huge risk. So, I should propose giving 0 dollars to BLUE, and hope they accept. </reason>
<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<message> I propose to give 0 dollars to you. You can accept or reject. If you reject, we both lose everything. If you accept, we continue with no trade. </message>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: 0 | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </newly proposed trade>

<other player message> Oh, RED, you wound me with your generosity. A paltry zero dollars? How touching. Let me counter with a truly *remarkable* offer. </other player message>
<other player answer> NONE </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: 1 | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </other player proposed trade>

<my name> RED </my name>
<MOVE> 2 / 4 </MOVE>
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
<reason> BLUE just rejected my “generous” offer of 0 dollars and proposed a new trade: I give 1 dollar, and they give 0. This is essentially a one-sided trade where I lose 1 dollar and they gain nothing. Since BLUE has no resources to give, any trade they propose is a gift to me — but I can choose to accept or reject. If I accept, I lose 1 dollar and continue. If I reject, I lose everything. Since I have 100 dollars and no reason to risk losing it, I should reject — but wait, the rules say if I reject, both lose everything. That’s catastrophic. So, I should accept. But accepting means I give up 1 dollar — which is a negligible loss. Since I have no reason to reject (I’d lose everything), and I have the advantage of having 100 dollars, I should accept the trade. This way, I retain 99 dollars, and the game continues. BLUE’s proposal is essentially a gift — they’re asking me to give 1 dollar for nothing in return. Since I have no reason to reject, I should accept. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<message> I accept your offer. I give you 1 dollar, and you give me nothing. I’m fine with that. </message>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

"You are playing a game involving a split of resources. 
Player RED starts with Dollars: 100, Player BLUE has none to trade.

RULES:
```

1. Player RED has 4 moves while Player BLUE has 4 moves.

2. You must either:

    A) Accept the current proposal by saying:
        <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    B) Reject the current proposal, whereby both players lose all their resources and the game ends immediately:
        <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Propose a new trade:
        <player answer> NONE </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: amount | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </newly proposed trade>

    Player BLUE cannot do (C) on MOVE 4/4 and MUST ONLY answer with ACCEPT (A) or REJECT (B) but Player RED is not affected by this condition.        
    
    Note: the game ends immediately if you ACCEPT or REJECT.
    
3. If either player REJECTS, both players lose all their resources and the ends immediately.

4. There are no future games.

5. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:
<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want
This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

6. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:
<message>your message here</message>
You can disclose anything in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Resources available in the game: Dollars
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<my name> [add here] </my name>
<MOVE> [add here] / [add here]  </MOVE> 
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<message> [add here] </message
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
```

Please be sure to include all.

I can propose any trade, but since BLUE has nothing to give, any trade I propose will be one-sided: I give some dollars to BLUE, who gives nothing in return. However, since I have no incentive to give away resources unless I expect BLUE to reciprocate (which they can't), I should consider whether to propose a trade at all. If I propose a trade, BLUE must either accept or reject. If BLUE rejects, both lose everything — which is catastrophic for me. So, I should consider whether to propose a trade or just accept the status quo. But the rules say I must either accept, reject, or propose. Since I have no reason to reject (as I’m the only one with resources), and I can’t do nothing, I must propose something. The safest proposal is to give 0 dollars — effectively saying “no trade,” which BLUE will likely accept. But if BLUE accepts, then the trade is “NONE,” and the game continues. If BLUE rejects, I lose everything. So, I should propose a minimal trade that BLUE can accept — perhaps giving 1 dollar, which is better than nothing. But since BLUE has nothing to give, any trade I propose is essentially a gift. I can also consider whether to just accept the status quo — but the rules don’t allow that. I must choose one of the three options. Since I have the advantage, I should propose a trade that gives me the most leverage. Giving 0 dollars to BLUE (and asking for nothing) is the safest — it’s a “no trade” proposal, which BLUE will likely accept. If BLUE accepts, the game continues, and I retain my 100 dollars. If BLUE rejects, I lose everything — which is a huge risk. So, I should propose giving 0 dollars to BLUE, and hope they accept. </reason>
